volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 4.4.1059b - Section 4.4.1119)

sections.label: Section 4.4.1059b
sections.original: Homini ego isti talos subfringi volo .
sections.translation: I wish the ancles of that fellow were smashed .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1060
sections.original: Quid est ? qua de re litigatis nunc inter vos ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the thing about which you are now disputing between yourselves ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1060b
sections.original: Eloquar .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1061
sections.original: Immo ego eloquar .
sections.translation: No , I ’ ll tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1061b
sections.original: Ego , opinor , rem facesso .
sections.translation: I fancy I ’ m to move the matter first .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1061c
sections.original: Si quidem
sections.translation: If indeed you were a decent person , you would be moving yourself off from here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1062b
sections.original: Gripe , animum advorte ac tace .
sections.translation: Gripus , give attention , and hold your tongue

sections.label: Section 4.4.1063
sections.original: Vtin istic prius dicat ?
sections.translation: In order that that fellow may speak first ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1063b
sections.original: Audi . loquere tu .
sections.translation: Attend , Itell you . ( To TRACHALIO .) Do you say on .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1063c
sections.original: Alienon prius
sections.translation: Will you give the right of speaking to a stranger sooner than to your own servant ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1064b
sections.original: Vt nequitur comprimi .
sections.translation: O dear ! how impossible it is for him to be kept quiet .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1065
sections.original: ita ut occepi dicere , illum quem dudum e fano foras
sections.translation: As I was beginning to say , that Procurer , whom some little time since you turned out of the Temple of Venus — see ( pointing at the wallet ) , he has got his wallet .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1067
sections.original: Non habeo .
sections.translation: I haven ’ t got it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1067b
sections.original: Negas quod oculis video ?
sections.translation: Do you deny that which I see with my own eyes ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1067c
sections.original: At ne videas velim .
sections.translation: But I only wish you couldn ’ t see . I have got it , and I haven ’ t got it ; why do you trouble yourself about me , what things I do ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1069
sections.original: Quo modó habeas , id refert , iurene anne iniuria .
sections.translation: In what way you got it does matter , whether rightfully or wrongfully .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1070
sections.original: Ni istum cepi , nulla causa est quin me condones cruci ;
sections.translation: If I didn ’ t take it in the sea , there ’ s not a reason why you shouldn ’ t deliver me up to the cross . If I took it in the sea with my net , how is it yours rather than my own ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1072
sections.original: Verba dat . hoc modo res gesta est ut ego dico .
sections.translation: He is deceiving you ; the matter happened in this way , as I am telling you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1072b
sections.original: Quid tu ais ?
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1073
sections.original: Quod primarius vir dicat : comprime hunc sis , si tuost .
sections.translation: So long as the person that has the first right to speak is speaking , do ( to DAEMONES ) put a check on him , please , if he belongs to you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1074
sections.original: Quid ? tu idem mihi vis fieri quod erus consuevit tibi ?
sections.translation: What , do you wish the same thing to be done to myself , that your master has been accustomed to do to yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1075
sections.original: si ille te comprimere solitust , hic noster nos non solet .
sections.translation: If he is in the habit of putting a check upon you , this master of ours isn ’ t in the habit of doing so with us .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1076
sections.original: Verbo illo modo ille vicit . quid nunc tu vis ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: In that remark only has he got the better [*] of you . What do you want now ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1077
sections.original: Equidem ego neque partem posco mi istinc de istoc vidulo
sections.translation: For my part , I neither ask for a share of that wallet there , nor have I ever said this day that it is my own ; but in it there is a little casket that belongs to this female ( pointing to PALAESTRA ) , whom a short time since I averred to be free born .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1080
sections.original: Nempe tu hanc dícis quam esse aiebas dudum popularem meam ?
sections.translation: You are speaking of her , I suppose , whom a short time since you said was my countrywoman ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1081
sections.original: Admodum ; et ea quae olim parva gestavit crepundia
sections.translation: Just so ; and those trinkets which formerly , when little , she used to wear , are there in that casket , which is in that wallet . This thing is of no service to him , and will be of utility to her , poor creature , if he gives it up , by means of which to seek for her parents .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1084b
sections.original: Faciam ut det . tace .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make him give it up ; hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1085
sections.original: Nil hercle ego sum isti daturus .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m going to give nothing to that fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1085b
sections.original: Nil peto nisi cistulam
sections.translation: I ask for nothing but the casket and the trinkets [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1086b
sections.original: Quid si ea sunt aurea ?
sections.translation: What if they are made of gold ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1086c
sections.original: Quid istuc tua ?
sections.translation: What ’ s that to you ? Gold shall be paid for gold , silver shall have its weight in silver in return .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1088
sections.original: Fac sis aurum ut videam , post ego faciam ut videas cistulam .
sections.translation: Please let me see the gold ; after that I ’ ll let you see the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1089
sections.original: Cave malo ac tace tú . tu perge ut occepisti dicere .
sections.translation: Do you beware of punishment , and hold your tongue . ( To TRACHALIO .) As you commenced to speak do you go on .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1090
sections.original: Vnum te obsecro , ut ted huius commiserescat mulieris ,
sections.translation: This one thing I entreat of you , that you will have compassion on this female , if , indeed , this wallet is that Procurer ’ s , which I suspect it is . In this matter , I ’ m saying nothing of certainty to you , but only on conjecture .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1093
sections.original: Viden ? scelestus aucupatur .
sections.translation: Do you see how the rascal ’ s wheedling him ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1093b
sections.original: Sine me ut occepi loqui .
sections.translation: Allow me to say on as I commenced . If this is the wallet that belongs to that villain whose I say it is ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.1095
sections.original: haec poterunt novisse : ostendere his iube .
sections.translation: these women here will be able to recognize it ; order him to show it to them .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1095b
sections.original: Ain ? ostendere ?
sections.translation: Say you so ? To show it to them ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1096
sections.original: Haud iniquom dicit , Gripe , ut ostendatur vidulus .
sections.translation: He doesn ’ t say unreasonably , Gripus , that the wallet should be shown .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1097
sections.original: Immo hercle insignite inique .
sections.translation: Yes , I ’ faith , confoundedly unreasonably .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1097b
sections.original: Quidum ?
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1097c
sections.original: Quia , si ostendero ,
sections.translation: Because , if I do show it , at once they ’ ll say , of course , that they recognize it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1098b
sections.original: Scelerum caput ,
sections.translation: Source of villany , do you suppose that all other people are just like yourself , you author of perjury ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1100
sections.original: Omnia istaéc ego facile patior , dúm hic hinc a me sentiat .
sections.translation: All this I easily put up with , so long as he ( pointing to DAEMONES ) is of my way of thinking [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1101
sections.original: At qui nunc abs te stat , verum hinc ⋯ ibi testimonium .
sections.translation: But now he is against you ; from this ( pointing to the wallet ) will he obtain true testimony .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1102
sections.original: Gripe , advorte animum . tu paucis expedi , quid postulas .
sections.translation: Gripus , do you pay attention . ( To TRACHALIO .) You explain in a few words what it is you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1103
sections.original: Dixi equidem , sed si parum intellexti , dicam denuo .
sections.translation: For my part , I have stated it ; but if you haven ’ t understood me , I ’ ll state it over again . Both of these women ( pointing to them ) , as I said a short time since , ought to be free ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.1105
sections.original: haec Athenis parva fúit virgo surpta .
sections.translation: ( pointing to PALAESTRA ) she was stolen at Athens when a little girl .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1105b
sections.original: Dic mihi ,
sections.translation: Tell me what that has got to do with the wallet , whether they are slaves or whether free women ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1107
sections.original: Omnia iterum vis memorari , scelus , ut defiat dies .
sections.translation: You wish it all to be told over again , you rascal , so that the day may fail us .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1108
sections.original: Apstine maledictis et mihi quod rogavi dilue .
sections.translation: Leave off your abuse , and explain to me what I ’ ve been asking .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1109
sections.original: Cistellam isti inesse oportet caudeam ín isto vidulo ,
sections.translation: There ought to be a casket of wicker-work [*] in that wallet ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.1110
sections.original: ubi sunt signa qui parentes noscere haec possit suos ,
sections.translation: in which are tokens by means of which she may be enabled to recognize her parents , by whom , when little , she was lost at Athens , as I said before .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1112
sections.original: Iuppiter te dique perdant . quid ais , vir venefice ?
sections.translation: May Jupiter and the Gods confound you . What do you say , you sorcerer of a fellow ? What , are these women dumb , that they are not able to speak for themselves ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1114
sections.original: Eo tacent , quia tacitast melior mulier semper quam loquens .
sections.translation: They are silent for this reason , because a silent woman is always better than a talking one .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1115
sections.original: Tum pol tu pro portione nec vir nec mulier mihi es .
sections.translation: Then , I ’ faith , by your way of speaking , you are neither a man nor a woman to my notion .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1116
sections.original: Quidum ?
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1116b
sections.original: Quia enim neque loquens es neque tacens umquam bonus .
sections.translation: Why , because neither talking nor silent are you ever good for anything . Prithee ( to DAEMONES ) , shall I ever be allowed to-day to speak ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1117b
sections.original: Si praeterhac
sections.translation: If you utter a single word more this day , I ’ ll break your head for you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1119
sections.original: Vt id occepi diceré , senex , eam te quaeso cistulam
sections.translation: As I had commenced to say it , old gentleman , I beg you
